metadata.title:
ARBITRATION


f|A1 RESULT
No More Buildings Until
Present Trouble Is


Settled

pages.raw_text:
The third day of the sympathetic
strike is here ana so far as can be
learned not many of the buildings
of the city have been forced to go
without the necessary amount of Ia
Dor to keep the work moving alone.
The meeting of the federal union.
with a large attendance, last night
throws a different light upon one
phase of the situation-no more new
buildings can be started by their aid
until the strike is settled


There We're about 200 members of
the federal union present last night,
but that is very small attendance
for the consideration of so great a
problem, when it is stated the union
is 500 strong.


Arbitration is now being talked of.
and the matter has got along to such
stage that it is very serious.


The master plumbers association
held meeting last night and the
strike from start to finish was re-
viewed and the determination to hold
out to the end again voiced by all
the members.


Thus far the strike has been con-
Quoted in an orderly manner by the
plumbers, there being only couple
of cases of violence reported to the
police. Since then many more non
union men have been put to work in
the strikers places, but these have
not been molested.


1t is not believed there will be any
violence shown since the building
trades have gone out in sympathy
with the plumbers. as arbitration has
already been talked of.


At the meeting of the painters
union last night at Labor hall it was
decided by a practically unanimous
vote to withdraw from the building
trades council. The painters, how
ever, decided to remain in the trades
council. There are over 100 members
of the painters union and their with
drawal from the building trades is
looked upon as long step in the di-
rection of settling the strike troubles.
